title: Asuka helping Shinji
wikitext:
[]: Asuka helping Shinji             Quote Post by Thomas68 » Mon Aug 11, 2014 5:19 pm After our [s:q30xdjol]retarded[/s:q30xdjol] unchancy main hero Shinji almost caused 4th Impact, EVA 13 was disabled and Shinji was captured. In the meantime, Shinji is in catatonia and our secondary loudmouth hero Asuka has taken him with her along with Rei to find the Spears. As some topics discuss how 4.0 will fix all this clusterfuck our hated King Anno created in 3.0, I wanted to discuss how on earth a [s:q30xdjol]nagging bitch[/s:q30xdjol] agressive person will help emotionally a [s:q30xdjol]insane[/s:q30xdjol] mentally unstabled passive person. This topic is about the actual state of mind of the characters and their personality and not how Shinji will try to fix [s:q30xdjol]if possible to fight alongside a army in tatters against a more powerful army that made them its fishbait in their plans countless times and could destroy them mercilessly[/s:q30xdjol] all the situation showed in 3.0. What do you think? Can Asuka help Shinji in some way or should Shinji be held to someone [s:q30xdjol]less psychotic and bitter[/s:q30xdjol] more stable like Misato? [s:q30xdjol]If she does even have time to deal with Shinji's insanity.[/s:q30xdjol]

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Mon Aug 11, 2014 5:23 pm We're now in the "drag Shinji around and do some exposition at him, before dying heroically in an act of self-sacrifice" part of the story, I'd say.

[]: Re: Asuka helping Shinji             Quote Post by pwhodges » Mon Aug 11, 2014 5:25 pm Thomas68 wrote: I wanted to discuss how on earth a [s]nagging bitch[/s] agressive person will help emotionally a [s]insane[/s] mentally unstabled passive person.

[]: Re: Asuka helping Shinji             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Mon Aug 11, 2014 5:49 pm Thomas68 wrote: In the meantime, Shinji is in catatonia and our secondary loudmouth hero Asuka has taken him with her along with Rei to find the Spears.

[]:             Quote Post by LightDragonman » Mon Aug 11, 2014 5:56 pm Yeah, it seems really ambiguous as to what's gonna happen. However, I will say that I don't think she'll be able to help Shinji by kicking him and throwing insults his way. All that's gonna do is just make him retreat further into his shell. It's less tough love and more kicking them when they're down. The way it is set up, she seems to be taking over the role Misato played in EOE. Heck, her dragging him through the wasteland mirrors Misato dragging him through NERV HQ. Bonus points if she gives the whole "so f****n what if I'm not you speech".

[]:             Quote Post by xyz346 » Mon Aug 11, 2014 6:46 pm Looking at the situation...Asuka probably wouldn't be the person to get Shinji out of his shell, in her state at the end of the film. More than likely she would do more harm than good if she made an effort to. Getting Shinji out of his shell would more likely be accomplished by Sakura or Misato. Asuka would be better suited to serve as a mentor to Shinji after he gets his act together.

[]:             Quote Post by Thomas68 » Mon Aug 11, 2014 6:50 pm LightDragonman wrote: Yeah, it seems really ambiguous as to what's gonna happen. However, I will say that I don't think she'll be able to help Shinji by kicking him and throwing insults his way. All that's gonna do is just make him retreat further into his shell. It's less tough love and more kicking them when they're down. The way it is set up, she seems to be taking over the role Misato played in EOE. Heck, her dragging him through the wasteland mirrors Misato dragging him through NERV HQ. Bonus points if she gives the whole "so f****n what if I'm not you speech".

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Mon Aug 11, 2014 7:08 pm Shikinami isn't Soryu. They'll be fine.

[]:             Quote Post by Sorrow » Mon Aug 11, 2014 7:49 pm xyz346 wrote: Looking at the situation...Asuka probably wouldn't be the person to get Shinji out of his shell, in her state at the end of the film. More than likely she would do more harm than good if she made an effort to. Getting Shinji out of his shell would more likely be accomplished by Sakura or Misato. Asuka would be better suited to serve as a mentor to Shinji after he gets his act together.

[]:             Quote Post by Chuckman » Mon Aug 11, 2014 7:55 pm If Asuka dies in a noble self sacrifice I will flip this table over.

[]:             Quote Post by Sorrow » Mon Aug 11, 2014 8:06 pm So it's good enough for Kaworu but not good enough for Asuka?

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Mon Aug 11, 2014 8:17 pm Soryu at least has a strong will to live, Shikinami doesn't seem too different. And in some ways, she's seemingly got her shit more together that's she's able to help the greater good, humanity. Something we never do (sure she helped in trying to save the world, but seemed more for personal reasons than anything "noble").

[]:             Quote Post by KingXanaduu » Mon Aug 11, 2014 8:25 pm Sailor Star Dust wrote: Soryu at least has a strong will to live, Shikinami doesn't seem too different. And in some ways, she's seemingly got her shit more together that's she's able to help the greater good, humanity. Something we never do (sure she helped in trying to save the world, but seemed more for personal reasons than anything "noble").

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Mon Aug 11, 2014 8:37 pm Soryu (during the MP Eva battle for instance) seemed to be fighting more for herself than humanity (and understandably so), while Shikinami was seemingly able to overcome whatever issues she had in the past 14 years to do what needs to be done in helping to save humanity. At least, that's what it so far seems like. Maybe Shikinami (like Misato) just does a really good job of holding her pain in.

[]:             Quote Post by LightDragonman » Mon Aug 11, 2014 8:54 pm Whatever happens to Shinji, I'm not quite sure Asuka's gonna be the only reason he'll snap out of it. Plus, she still has the MP Evas to deal with, if she even goes against them at all this time that is. Though, I would find it funny if her fight against them ends up resembling this (it already sort of does in the preview shown after 3.33's credits). [url]https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=ItvlEO_-V-g[/url]

[]:             Quote Post by TMBounty_Hunter » Mon Aug 11, 2014 10:05 pm It's simple. Anno recently told us that Final will be mathy. Asuka is love. Shinji is love. Do the math and Final will end with SEKIHA LOVE-LOVE TENKYOKEN

[]:             Quote Post by UrsusArctos » Mon Aug 11, 2014 11:13 pm Wrong thread, TMB. Anyway, with 14 years gone and a whole lot of rage bottled up inside Shiki, having any kind of romance between her and Shinji is now out.

[]:             Quote Post by Chuckman » Mon Aug 11, 2014 11:24 pm But, all you need is love.

[]:             Quote Post by Ray » Tue Aug 12, 2014 1:57 am What I want to know is, Why? Why would Asuka help Shinji after everything he's done? She lost her eye because he would not fight Bardiel (that is the name right?), he destroyed Nerv headquarters and possibly the rest of Japan, deserted Wille and Fraternized with the enemy, set off ANOTHER impact because he would not listen. Etc. On top of all this he's been gone for 14 years and hes been nothing but a bad hate filled memory for her for at least that long. Why help him? I'm not one for giving up, and he's obviously not going to die. but to paraphrase a comic book I read once. "Wouldn't it be more pragmatic and far more merciful for Asuka to simply put this poor suffering creature out of his misery?" so why didn't she?

[]:             Quote Post by pwhodges » Tue Aug 12, 2014 2:05 am And why did she call his name in the first scene? That alone is enough to make the point that pure hatred is not a sufficient summary of her feelings about him.
